findings.title: Actual NUL byte breaks Go source parsing
findings.evidence: The added string literal contains an actual U+0000 character between `Hello` and `World`. Go source files cannot contain NUL characters, so this line will fail to compile instead of printing the intended text.
findings.suggested_comment: This line appears to include a literal NUL byte in the source file. Go rejects U+0000 in source, so this will break the build. Please encode it explicitly, for example with `"Hello\x00World"`, if the output needs to contain a null byte.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the embedded NUL character with an escaped representation such as `\x00`, `\u0000`, or remove it if it was accidental.
